build.description: Build the production desktop controller bundle (alias for `desktop:build`)

check.description: Full workspace gate: everything in check:static + check:rust. CI splits these — check:static once, check:rust per-OS in a matrix — but locally `run check` does both

cases.linux: if ! command -v cargo-xwin >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo '==> cargo-xwin not installed — skipping the Windows (msvc) cross-check. Enable it once with: cargo install --locked cargo-xwin'; exit 0; fi; LLVM_BIN=; if command -v llvm-lib >/dev/null 2>&1; then LLVM_BIN=$(dirname "$(command -v llvm-lib)"); else for cfg in llvm-config llvm-config-21 llvm-config-20 llvm-config-19 llvm-config-18 llvm-config-17 llvm-config-16; do if command -v "$cfg" >/dev/null 2>&1; then d=$("$cfg" --bindir); if [ -x "$d/llvm-lib" ]; then LLVM_BIN=$d; break; fi; fi; done; fi; if [ -z "$LLVM_BIN" ]; then echo '==> cargo-xwin is installed but no LLVM llvm-lib archiver was found — install the llvm package (provides llvm-lib). Aborting the Windows cross-check.' >&2; exit 1; fi; rustup target add x86_64-pc-windows-msvc >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; echo "==> Windows (msvc) cross-check via cargo-xwin — LLVM: $LLVM_BIN"; OPENH264_NO_ASM=1 PATH="$LLVM_BIN:$PATH" cargo xwin clippy -p libretether-agent -p libretether-relay -p libretether-protocol -p libretether-common --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --all-targets -- -D warnings

commands.default: echo native clippy above is the {{ RUN.os }} gate here - no windows cross-check needed

check:rust.description: OS-dependent gate: clippy the whole Rust workspace (CI runs this on Linux, macOS and Windows so per-OS code — the Windows encoder, macOS capture, etc. — can't rot). On Linux it ALSO cross-clippies the shippable non-desktop crates for x86_64-pc-windows-msvc via cargo-xwin, so Windows-only agent/relay code is linted locally without a Windows box (needs `cargo install cargo-xwin` + clang/LLVM; OPENH264_NO_ASM skips nasm and the LLVM bindir is auto-discovered). Skips cleanly when cargo-xwin isn't installed — e.g. the CI Linux leg, where the windows-latest leg is the real Windows gate. The desktop (Tauri) crate is left to the native Windows leg.

check:static.description: OS-independent gate: JS/TS lint, TypeScript type-check, rustfmt check, and license-check (results don't vary by OS, so CI runs this once)

clean.description: Remove build artifacts (Rust target, node_modules, dist)

lint.description: Auto-format & fix the whole workspace (Biome for JS/CSS/JSON, rustfmt for Rust)

commands: git commit libretether-desktop/package.json Cargo.toml libretether-desktop/src-tauri/tauri.conf.json Cargo.lock -m {{ shell_quote(concat('chore: release ', VAR.tag)) }}
release.description: Bump the version (major/minor/patch) across manifests, refresh Cargo.lock, commit, tag v<new>, then push the tag BEFORE the branch — release-desktop.yml triggers on the branch push and checks out the tag, so the tag must already exist when that build starts

linux: sudo apt-get install -y libwebkit2gtk-4.1-dev build-essential curl wget file libxdo-dev libssl-dev libayatana-appindicator3-dev librsvg2-dev libxcb1-dev libxrandr-dev libxfixes-dev libdbus-1-dev libpipewire-0.3-dev libxkbcommon-dev clang llvm freerdp3-x11 nasm

commands.default: echo 'No system setup step for {{ RUN.os }} — install the Tauri prerequisites manually: https://tauri.app/start/prerequisites/'

setup.description: Install system prerequisites (Tauri/WebKit deps) and JS dependencies (husky hooks are set up by the package.json prepare script). On Linux it also installs the Windows cross-compile toolchain used by `check:rust` — `llvm` (provides the llvm-lib archiver + llvm-config for auto-discovery) and cargo-xwin — so the msvc cross-clippy runs after setup with no extra steps. cargo-xwin fetches the MSVC SDK itself on first use; only add `lld` by hand if you later want to cross-*build* .exe (linking), not just clippy.

test.description: Run the whole Rust workspace's tests

test:headless.description: Workspace tests minus the desktop crate. The Windows CI leg uses this: a test binary that links the Tauri/webview stack fails to load outside a bundled app there (WebView2Loader.dll import → STATUS_ENTRYPOINT_NOT_FOUND). Desktop tests still run on the Linux/macOS legs, and Windows still compile-checks them via clippy --all-targets

update-deps.description: Update JS dependencies to their latest versions and update Cargo crates
